notes: Run 2026-06-26 (category 2 of 2). Active count was 45 — tied with literature_books for second-thinnest eligible category. Under-weight tiers: $800 (1 active!) and $1000 (3 active). Pack heavily weighted toward those tiers: 2x $200, 3x $400, 3x $600, 5x $800, 2x $1000. Fills gaps in Basketball (Bill Russell, Magic Johnson, Julius Erving), Tennis & Golf (Roger Federer, Augusta National, Tiger Woods), Soccer (Marta), Olympics (Curling, Heptathlon), Baseball (Boston Red Sox, Derek Jeter), and minor gaps in Board Games, Rules & Terminology, and Sports Records. Hard tail: Bill Russell ($1000, 11-ring dynasty from oblique franchise angle) + Marta Vieira da Silva ($1000, first footballer of any gender at 5 consecutive World Cups).

questions.clue: In this winter Olympic sport, teams of four players take turns sliding polished granite stones toward a circular target — called 'the house' — on a sheet of ice, while two teammates sweep with brooms to guide the stone's path.

citations.title: Curling
citations.snippet: Curling is a sport in which players slide stones on a sheet of ice towards a target area that is segmented into four concentric circles. It is related to bowls, boules, and shuffleboard. Two teams, each with four players, take turns sliding

questions.clue: Introduced in 1978 by Milton Bradley, this boisterous tabletop children's game has two to four players pressing levers to make their animal models gobble as many marbles as possible from a central pool.

citations.title: Hungry Hungry Hippos
citations.snippet: Hungry Hungry Hippos is a tabletop game made for 2–4 players, produced by Hasbro, under the brand of its subsidiary, Milton Bradley. The idea for the game was published in 1967 by toy inventor Fred Kroll and it was introduced in 1978. The o

questions.clue: In this casino game, a croupier sends a ball spinning inside a bowl against a rotating numbered wheel; players bet on which pocket the ball will ultimately settle in, wagering on color, number, or grouping.

citations.title: Roulette
citations.snippet: Roulette is a casino game which was likely developed from the Italian game Biribi. In the game, a player may choose to place a bet on a single number, various groupings of numbers, the color red or black, whether the number is odd or even,

questions.clue: Two teams of eleven players use curved sticks to propel a small, hard ball into the opposing goal in this fast-paced team sport, which is played on artificial turf and is simply called by its non-qualifier name across Asia, Oceania, and parts of Europe.

citations.title: Field hockey

questions.clue: This AL East franchise, which has called Fenway Park home since 1912, endured an 86-year World Series drought — attributed to the sale of a star player to a bitter rival — before finally winning the championship in 2004.

citations.title: Boston Red Sox

questions.clue: Nicknamed 'the Captain,' this New York Yankees shortstop spent his entire 20-year career with one club, earned five World Series rings, and set the franchise record for career hits while finishing sixth all-time in MLB history.

citations.title: Derek Jeter

questions.clue: Contested over two days at major track-and-field competitions, this women's combined event encompasses seven disciplines and has been a regular Olympic program entry since the 1984 Los Angeles Games.

citations.title: Heptathlon
citations.snippet: A heptathlon is a track and field combined events contest made up of seven events. The name derives from the Greek ἑπτά and ἄθλος. A competitor in a heptathlon is referred to as a heptathlete.

questions.clue: Composed of 32 teams across the United States and Canada, this professional league awards the Stanley Cup — the oldest professional sports trophy in North America — to its annual playoff champion.

citations.title: National Hockey League

questions.clue: After leading Michigan State to the 1979 NCAA championship, this player was selected first overall by the Los Angeles Lakers and went on to be widely regarded as the greatest point guard the NBA has ever seen.

questions.clue: Turning professional in 1996 at age 20, this golfer won his first major the following April by a record 12 strokes and ultimately tied the all-time PGA Tour wins record while standing second only to Jack Nicklaus in career major championships.

citations.title: Tiger Woods

questions.clue: This forward won three championships and four MVP awards in the American Basketball Association, helping legitimize the rival league as its biggest star before the ABA merged with the NBA in 1976.

citations.title: Julius Erving

questions.clue: He surpassed Pete Sampras's record of 14 men's Grand Slam titles at Wimbledon in 2009 and ultimately won a record eight championships at that tournament before retiring in September 2022.

citations.title: Roger Federer

questions.clue: Designed by Scottish architect Alister MacKenzie for the retired amateur champion Bobby Jones and opened in 1932, this private course in Georgia has hosted an invitational major every April since 1934.

citations.title: Augusta National Golf Club
citations.snippet: Augusta National Golf Club is a golf club in Augusta, Georgia, United States. It is known for hosting the annual Masters Tournament.

questions.clue: The first footballer of any gender to score at five consecutive FIFA World Cups, this Brazilian forward holds the all-time Women's World Cup goals record with 17 and has been named FIFA Women's Player of the Year six times.

questions.clue: During his 13 seasons with one NBA franchise, this center accumulated eleven championship rings — more than any player in league history — and was the centerpiece of the most decorated dynasty the sport has known.
